title: The Secret Lives of Objects
description:
There is a particular pleasure in lost objects. We know it in ordinary life: the small shock of finding again what had vanished, the almost absurd intensity of a key, a ring, a photograph, a letter suddenly returned to us. Natalie Lemle’s Artifacts, her debut novel, turns that intimate sensation into a larger, more dangerous […]

The post The Secret Lives of Objects appeared first on
hook: Objects return to us charged with almost absurd intensity—a debut novel that makes lost things feel like they matter.

title: The Canadian poet Margaret Avison lived with such intense inwardness that Northrop Frye called her “a conscientious objector to life”
description: The Canadian poet Margaret Avison lived with such intense inwardness that Northrop Frye called her “a conscientious objector to life”
hook: Margaret Avison's poetry was so inward Northrop Frye called her a conscientious objector to life itself.

title: Sabri Sundos Faces Catastrophe With Flowers
description: “I am obsessed with autonomy and movement,” the Palestinian-American artist tells Hyperallergic in an interview.
hook: Palestinian-American artist Sabri Sundos uses flowers to face catastrophe—an interview on autonomy and movement as survival.

title: The AI Debate Bores Me
description:
Where is the joy? Where is the pride in ChatGPT or Claude? AI stinks of the living dead.

The post The AI Debate Bores Me appeared first on Harper's Magazine.
hook: John R. MacArthur on why AI discourse feels like watching the living dead—where's the actual joy or stakes?
